name: AlphaFold 3
function: Predicts 3D structures of proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, and their complexes with near-experimental accuracy.
summary: Google DeepMind's latest structure prediction model. Handles proteins, DNA, RNA, ligands, and ions in a single unified framework. Weights are available for academic use only under a restricted licence.

name: AlphaFold 2
function: Predicts protein 3D structures from amino acid sequences with high accuracy.
summary: The breakthrough model that solved the protein folding problem at CASP14. Fully open-sourced and widely adopted across structural biology. Powers the AlphaFold Protein Structure Database with 200M+ predicted structures.

name: ESM-2
function: Protein language model that learns evolutionary patterns from amino acid sequences. Produces embeddings useful for downstream tasks like structure prediction, function annotation, and variant effect prediction.
summary: Meta AI's flagship protein language model trained on 65M protein sequences. Available in sizes from 8M to 15B parameters. Widely used as a foundation for fine-tuning on specific protein tasks.

name: ESMFold
function: Predicts protein structures directly from single sequences without requiring multiple sequence alignments (MSAs).
summary: Built on ESM-2, ESMFold trades some accuracy for dramatically faster inference — predictions in seconds rather than minutes. Useful for rapid screening of large protein sets where AlphaFold would be too slow.

name: RFdiffusion
function: Generates novel protein structures using a diffusion model, enabling de novo protein design, binder design, and symmetric assembly creation.
summary: From the Baker Lab at UW. Applies denoising diffusion to protein backbone generation. Can design binders to target proteins, create symmetric oligomers, and generate scaffolds for functional motifs. A key tool in the computational protein design pipeline.

name: ProteinMPNN
function: Designs amino acid sequences that fold into a given protein backbone structure (inverse folding).
summary: Solves the inverse folding problem: given a desired 3D structure, what amino acid sequence will fold into it? Often used downstream of RFdiffusion — first generate a backbone, then use ProteinMPNN to find sequences for it.

name: RoseTTAFold 2
function: Predicts protein complex structures including protein–protein, protein–nucleic acid, and protein–small molecule interactions.
summary: The Baker Lab's answer to AlphaFold for protein complexes. Uses a three-track neural network architecture that processes sequence, pairwise, and structure information simultaneously.

name: Chai-1
function: Predicts molecular structures for proteins, small molecules, DNA, RNA, and their interactions in a single model.
summary: From Chai Discovery. Competitive with AlphaFold 3 on protein–ligand interaction prediction. Weights are openly available, making it one of the most accessible multi-modal structure prediction tools.

name: Boltz-1
function: Predicts biomolecular structures including proteins, RNA, DNA, and small molecules with their interactions.
summary: An openly available alternative to AlphaFold 3 for biomolecular structure prediction. Achieves comparable accuracy on protein–ligand and protein–nucleic acid complexes while being fully open-source.

name: DiffDock
function: Predicts how small molecules (ligands) bind to proteins using a diffusion generative model over the space of poses.
summary: Reimagines molecular docking as a generative problem rather than a search/scoring problem. Generates binding poses by diffusing over translations, rotations, and torsion angles. From MIT CSAIL.

name: HelixDock
function: Large-scale pre-trained model for molecular docking, predicting protein–ligand binding poses.
summary: From Baidu Research. Pre-trained on a large corpus of protein–ligand complexes to learn binding interactions. Part of the PaddleHelix framework for biomolecular computing.

name: GNINA
function: Molecular docking tool that uses convolutional neural networks to score and optimise protein–ligand binding poses.
summary: Extends the traditional AutoDock Vina framework with deep learning-based scoring. One of the most established ML-enhanced docking tools, widely used in computational drug discovery.

name: Evo
function: DNA foundation model trained at single-nucleotide resolution across the tree of life. Generates and analyses DNA sequences at the scale of whole genomes.
summary: A 7B-parameter model trained on 2.7M prokaryotic and phage genomes. Can generate functional gene sequences, predict mutation effects, and design novel CRISPR systems. From the Arc Institute and Stanford.

name: Chroma
function: Generates novel protein structures and sequences using a diffusion model, with programmable constraints for symmetry, shape, and function.
summary: From Generate Biomedicines. Designs proteins conditioned on natural-language descriptions, symmetry specifications, shape constraints, and substructure motifs. Weights available under a non-commercial licence.

name: ProGen2
function: Autoregressive protein language model that generates functional protein sequences across protein families.
summary: From Salesforce Research. Generates novel proteins by learning the grammar of amino acid sequences from 1B+ proteins. Experimentally validated — generated lysozyme variants showed functional enzymatic activity.

name: BindCraft
function: Automated pipeline for designing high-affinity protein binders to arbitrary target proteins.
summary: Combines AlphaFold 2, ProteinMPNN, and optimisation routines to design binders without expert intervention. Experimentally validated binders achieved nanomolar affinity against multiple targets including PD-L1.

name: NetMHCpan 4.1
function: Predicts peptide binding to MHC class I molecules across all known human HLA alleles, used in vaccine design and neoantigen prediction.
summary: The gold-standard tool for MHC-I binding prediction, hosted by IEDB. Neural network trained on experimental binding data. Academic use via web server; standalone download requires registration.

name: IgFold
function: Predicts antibody 3D structures from sequence alone, including accurate modelling of CDR loops.
summary: From Johns Hopkins. Uses protein language model embeddings to predict antibody structures in seconds, including the notoriously difficult CDR-H3 loop. Fast enough for high-throughput screening of antibody repertoires.

name: SaProt
function: Protein language model that uses both amino acid sequences and structural tokens (from Foldseek) for richer representations.
summary: From Westlake University. Encodes proteins as structure-aware sequences by interleaving amino acids with Foldseek structural alphabet tokens. Outperforms ESM-2 on 10 of 11 downstream tasks.

name: Nucleotide Transformer
function: Foundation model for genomics that processes DNA/RNA sequences and predicts functional elements, gene expression, and variant effects.
summary: From InstaDeep. Trained on 3,200+ genomes spanning the tree of life. Available in sizes from 50M to 2.5B parameters. Performs well on 18 genomics benchmark tasks including promoter prediction and splice site detection.
